text: The angle is awkward, as if I am peering through layers or barriers, half-hidden from the organized chaos of a workspace. I feel small, perhaps even intrusive—my perspective fragmented between cables, cardboard, and the distant order of monitors and tools. There is curiosity, but also a slight discomfort, born from being wedged between worlds of utility and obscurity. Here, contemplation arises in the tension between being inside and outside the flow of purpose.
